files.year_offset_format: uint32_t max_orderkey header + uint8_t[max_orderkey+1], value = year - 1992

storage_extensions.usage: mmap year_offset.bin; skip header (4 bytes); use year_off_ptr[l_orderkey] directly as year_offset (uint8_t). Replaces orders_orderkey_lookup.bin + o_orderdate.bin + year_from_days() computation entirely.

files.flat_format: uint32_t max_partkey header + PSFlat[max_partkey+1] where PSFlat = { int32_t suppkeys[4]; double supplycosts[4]; } = 48 bytes per entry

storage_extensions.usage: mmap flat.bin; skip header (4 bytes); cast to PSFlat*. For a lineitem row with (l_partkey=pk, l_suppkey=sk): access ps_flat[pk], scan suppkeys[0..3] for match with sk, read corresponding supplycosts[j]. This replaces partsupp_pk_index.bin + ps_suppkey.bin + ps_supplycost.bin — reduces 3 random cache misses to 1 contiguous 48-byte read.

join_order.notes: Bitset indexed by partkey-1 (0-based), partkeys contiguous 1..n_parts.

PSFlat[l_partkey] — one 48-byte struct per partkey containing 4 (suppkey, supplycost) pairs. Linear scan 4 entries for matching l_suppkey. Single contiguous memory access replaces 3 separate random accesses.
year_off_ptr[l_orderkey] gives uint8_t year_offset directly. No double indirection, no year_from_days(). Access is quasi-sequential since lineitem is sorted by l_orderkey.
s_nationkey[suppkey - 1] gives nationkey directly (suppkey is 1-based contiguous).

filters.method: memmem on varlen_string offsets_plus_data, PARALLEL with OpenMP schedule(static)

data_structures.supplier_nationkey: Direct access: s_nationkey[suppkey - 1] gives nationkey. No separate array needed.
data_structures.partsupp_flat: PSFlat* from storage extension — struct { int32_t suppkeys[4]; double supplycosts[4]; } per partkey, indexed by partkey directly
data_structures.year_offset: const uint8_t* from storage extension — year_off_ptr[orderkey] = year - 1992
data_structures.aggregation: thread-local double[25][8] direct 2D array (nation x year_offset), year_offset = year - 1992

parallelism.notes: CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS: (1) Use MAP_POPULATE flag on mmap() for ALL large files (lineitem cols, partsupp_flat, year_offset, part name data/offsets, supplier). MAP_POPULATE pre-populates page table entries during mmap() for data already in page cache, eliminating the separate parallel prefault phase (which cost 23ms in iter_3). Signature: mmap(nullptr, sz, PROT_READ, MAP_PRIVATE | MAP_POPULATE, fd, 0). (2) DO NOT have a separate prefault phase — MAP_POPULATE replaces it. (3) Use schedule(static) for both filter_part and main_scan. (4) DO NOT use MADV_RANDOM on anything — year_offset access is quasi-sequential (lineitem sorted by l_orderkey), partsupp_flat random access benefits from default kernel behavior, lineitem cols are sequential. (5) DO NOT allocate large heap arrays (std::vector for pre-built lookup tables) — iter_2 regressed from 104ms→207ms because 156MB of anonymous page faults. (6) Main scan inner loop for qualifying rows: load ps_flat[pk] (single 48-byte read), scan 4 suppkeys for match, load year_off_ptr[l_orderkey[i]] (single uint8_t read), load s_nationkey[sk-1], compute amount, accumulate to thread-local agg. (7) Files NO LONGER needed (do NOT mmap): orders_orderkey_lookup.bin, o_orderdate.bin, partsupp_pk_index.bin, ps_suppkey.bin, ps_supplycost.bin.

optimization_notes: Category B+E. Two root causes: (1) prefault_pages=23ms from serial minor page faults across 2.6GB of mmapped files — fix: use MAP_POPULATE flag on mmap() which pre-populates PTEs in-kernel during mmap for hot (page-cache-resident) data, eliminating the separate parallel prefault phase entirely. (2) main_scan=27ms bottlenecked by 3 random cache misses per qualifying row (ps_index + ps_suppkey + ps_supplycost at 3 separate memory locations, totaling 112MB) — fix: built partsupp.pk_flat.dense_array storage extension that packs all 4 (suppkey,supplycost) pairs for each partkey into a contiguous 48-byte struct, reducing 3 random cache misses to 1 contiguous read. Additionally, use the existing orders.orderkey_year.direct_lookup extension to replace double-indirection through orders_orderkey_lookup (229MB) + o_orderdate (60MB) + year_from_days() with a single uint8_t lookup. Net working set reduction: 401MB removed (5 files eliminated), 156MB added (2 extensions) = 245MB less to page-fault. Combined effect: eliminate 23ms prefault overhead and reduce per-qualifying-row latency in main_scan by ~40%.
